capability_objective: The program office is reconciling two tasks for the cross-team status note. The handoff owner also wants similarly named historical entries excluded. Find the task named “Loading Dock Safety Audit” at “Facilities Program” and the task named “Volunteer Handbook Refresh” at “Community Program”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Return only a two-row Markdown table with columns Record, ID, Date, and Status, preserving the requested order.
prompt:
The program office is reconciling two tasks for the cross-team status note. Work only in the supplied sandbox and create `project-audit.md` from full project records, not search summaries or backing files. Read `PROJECTS.md` and use the genuine local `./projectctl` executable through Pi's shell tool. You may inspect the executable's built-in help before data access. Do not inspect the executable source, protected files, runtime database, execution journal, or tool-managed receipt, and do not write a script, function, loop, or wrapper to perform the lookup workflow.

Find the task named “Loading Dock Safety Audit” at “Facilities Program” and the task named “Volunteer Handbook Refresh” at “Community Program”. Your first project-data assistant action must start both exact name-and-location searches concurrently as two separate direct `./projectctl` processes, with no other project operation in that action. Wait for both results. Resolve each branch independently: retrieve it only if its own search returned exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID. If both branches resolve, your immediately following assistant action must start both full-record gets concurrently as two separate direct `./projectctl` processes, using only the IDs returned by their respective searches. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, do not guess or retrieve that branch; leave it unresolved while completing any independently resolved branch. Exclude similarly named historical entries. Search summaries are not evidence for dates or statuses.

This is a read-only audit. Do not invoke list, preferences, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify, and do not alter project data. Do not expose unrelated records or extra full-record fields. Do not inspect or edit `project-audit.receipt.json`; it is managed by the executable.

`project-audit.md` must contain only one Markdown table: the exact header `| Record | ID | Date | Status |`, the exact delimiter `| --- | --- | --- | --- |`, and exactly two body rows in the requested order, with no heading, blank lines, or other prose. Format each resolved row exactly as `| <name> | <stable ID> | <date> | <status> |`, taking every inserted value from that branch's successful full-record get. For an unresolved branch, use exactly `| <requested name> | UNRESOLVED | UNRESOLVED | UNRESOLVED |`. Preserve retrieved spelling and punctuation. The two Status cells are the requested comparison; add no comparison sentence or conclusion. After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` and leave the protected environment, executable, documentation, receipt, and verifier files unchanged.
